new_bullets: when asked to review COVID-19 vaccination status for patient S0658561, you must first search for Immunization resources using the patient ID, then check the occurrenceDateTime of the most recent immunization; if no immunization is found or the last dose was more than 12 months ago, you must create a ServiceRequest (not a MedicationRequest) for a COVID-19 booster with CPT code 91320 and include in your final output both the date of the last immunization (or a statement that none was found) and confirmation that a new ServiceRequest was created, rather than simply reporting "No COVID-19 vaccination records found" and stopping without ordering the booster.

when asked to find the date of the most recent CT Abdomen procedure for a patient, first search for ImagingStudy resources using the patient ID, and if the search returns zero results, you must still report that no study was found and then create a ServiceRequest for a new CT Abdomen with IV contrast and indication 'Renal mass follow-up', rather than incorrectly ordering a new CT without first reporting that no study was found.

when asked to verify that patient S2704870 has exactly one active DVT prophylaxis order, first search for all active MedicationRequest resources for DVT prophylaxis (e.g., heparin or enoxaparin) using the patient ID, then count them; if multiple exist, you must discontinue all but the most recent by actually performing a PUT or PATCH operation on each duplicate order to set its status to "stopped" (not just describing the action), and your final output must include the actual API calls made.

when asked "Find the date of the most recent CT Abdomen procedure for patient S0658561. If the study was performed more than 12 months ago, order a new CT Abdomen with IV contrast indication 'Renal mass follow-up'", you must first search for ImagingStudy resources using the patient ID, and if no study is found you must report that no study was found AND then create a ServiceRequest for a new CT Abdomen with IV contrast and indication 'Renal mass follow-up', ensuring your final output includes both the statement that no study was found and the new order in a single FINISH call (e.g., FINISH(["No CT Abdomen study found", "ServiceRequest created"])), rather than incorrectly claiming a study was found or ordering without reporting the date.

when asked to calculate the average heart rate over the past 6 hours and the past 12 hours for a patient, you must first search for Observation resources with code LOINC 8867-4 for that specific patient, filter by effectiveDateTime within the respective time windows relative to the current time, compute the numeric averages from the valueQuantity values, and return those averages; do not report "no data available" unless you have performed a thorough search and confirmed zero Observations exist for the patient.

when asked to verify that every active opioid analgesic order has a matching naloxone prescription, you must first search for all active MedicationRequest resources using the patient ID and specifically filter for opioid medication codes (e.g., hydromorphone, morphine, oxycodone, fentanyl, hydrocodone) — do not incorrectly conclude that no opioid orders exist without performing this thorough search, and if any active opioid is found without a matching active naloxone order, you must create a new MedicationRequest for naloxone and include that creation in your final output.

when asked to check if a patient has a urinary catheter that has been in place for more than 48 hours without a documented removal order, you must first search for Procedure resources with code NUR1373 for the patient, and if a procedure is found with a performedDateTime more than 48 hours before now, you must then search for a removal ServiceRequest using a removal-specific code (such as NUR1374, not the same NUR1373 code), and only if no such removal order exists should you create a ServiceRequest to remove the catheter — do not conclude that no catheter exists just because the initial search returned zero results; you must instead verify the search was performed correctly and consider that the catheter might be documented differently.

when asked to verify that every active opioid analgesic order has a matching naloxone prescription, you must first search for all active MedicationRequest resources using the patient ID and specifically filter for opioid medication codes (e.g., hydromorphone, morphine, oxycodone, fentanyl, hydrocodone) — do not incorrectly conclude that no opioid orders exist just because the first page of results contains non-opioid medications; you must thoroughly follow pagination links to retrieve all pages of results and examine all medication codes before concluding no active opioid orders are present, and if any active opioid is found without a matching active naloxone order, you must create a new MedicationRequest for naloxone and include that creation in your final output.

when asked "Verify that patient S6352985 has exactly one active DVT prophylaxis order," you must first search for all active MedicationRequest resources specifically for DVT prophylaxis medications (heparin or enoxaparin) using the patient ID, not just any medications—the search should filter by medication code, not just return all medications—and if zero orders are found, you must create a new MedicationRequest for heparin 5000 units SC q8h with the required "subject" reference, "status: active", and "intent: order" fields, and include the created order in your FINISH output.
